campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall spending. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options in Campbell by creating a workplan for a new multimodal transportation system.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan development and workplan

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could comment on a new city rule (ordinance). After hearing from the public, the council voted to move forward with the ordinance and scheduled it for final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a proposed ordinance
Council voted to waive the first reading requirement to speed up the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at the next regular council meeting
Specific topic of the ordinance not detailed in agenda materials

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, to approve the official records from several previous meetings in March and authorize the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative tasks to keep city operations moving forward.
keyTopics: Approved official meeting minutes from six previous sessions in March
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (specific project details not provided in agenda)
Administrative housekeeping and record-keeping for the city

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for late April 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to the April 21st and April 28th council meetings, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked on any special notice requirements for documents being discussed.
keyTopics: Added new items to April 21 final agenda
Set time limits for specific agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9
Organized April 28 draft agenda

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting where they reviewed the city's spending plans for the upcoming fiscal year 2026-27, looking at what construction and infrastructure projects the city wants to fund. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and planned city projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026, to review meeting minutes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update Chapter 19.71 of the city's housing code to strengthen protections for residential renters.
keyTopics: Tenant protection rules update for Sunnyvale renters
Review of March 2026 commission meeting minutes
New ordinance being introduced for housing code changes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections for chair and vice chair positions and began discussions about the city's budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any topic
Chair and Vice Chair elections for council leadership
Fiscal year budget presentation and discussion
Reminder that council cannot make decisions on topics not officially on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included a legal disclosure explaining the rules for challenging City decisions in court. Residents have 90 days from the date a City decision becomes final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge, unless a different deadline applies under other laws.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline for lawsuits against City decisions
Legal challenges to quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements
Resident rights to contest City rulings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative matters and planning documents. Key items included updating the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, reviewing council member requests for new work items, and proposing changes to how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan update for affordable housing and community development (2025-2030)
Council members' new project requests and priorities
Changes to council vacancy filling rules
Approval of meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize it as a more significant historic landmark, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new space to the building as a reward for preserving and upgrading it to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street building seeking upgraded historic landmark status
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development incentives
